original_hypothesis.title: Marketplace freeze can permanently lock listed vestings due to isFreeze modifier on unlist path
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: SecondSwap_Marketplace applies the isFreeze modifier to unlistVesting(). If marketplaceSetting.isMarketplaceFreeze() is set to true, neither sellers nor admin can unlist, so vesting allocations already escrowed in VestingManager/StepVesting remain stuck and cannot be returned to sellers until unfreezing. If the admin account is compromised, misconfigured, or intentionally freezes indefinitely, funds are effectively locked.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete state-machine dead-end: a safety mechanism (freeze) disables the primary exit hatch (unlisting/withdrawal), violating typical emergency-design expectations that users can always exit during pause/freeze.
deployment_assumptions: Admin (s2Admin) sets isMarketplaceFreeze = true for extended period (malice, compromise, or operational error)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: SecondSwap_MarketplaceSetting.setMarketplaceStatus(bool) exists and is onlyAdmin; SecondSwap_Marketplace.unlistVesting is guarded by isFreeze.

proof_of_signal.pattern: modifier isFreeze requires !isMarketplaceFreeze; applied to unlistVesting, blocking exit while frozen

preconditions: There exist active listings with vesting balances escrowed in VestingManager
No alternative emergency withdrawal function exists elsewhere

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: pause/freeze blocks both entry (list/buy) and exit (unlist)
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: listed vestings stuck in escrow while frozen
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no 'allow unlist while frozen' or emergency withdraw path

analysis_questions: Does any other contract provide a recovery path to return escrowed vestings while marketplace is frozen?
Is freeze intended as 'maintenance mode' only, or as emergency pause (should allow withdrawals)?
What is the governance/process for unfreezing?
validation_requirements: State-machine review: simulate listing -> freeze -> attempt unlist/purchase
Confirm VestingManager cannot be used directly by seller (onlyMarketplace)
Assess operational risk and propose safe emergency design if needed

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: After many users list vestings, admin freezes marketplace. Sellers cannot unlist (exit) and buyers cannot purchase, leaving vesting allocations stuck in VestingManager until admin unfreezes.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: High—loss of liquidity and potential inability to recover escrowed allocations during extended freeze
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All sellers with active listings at time of freeze
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Low/Medium—requires admin to unfreeze; otherwise stuck

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests mentioned for freeze behavior on user exits

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: monitor freeze flag toggles and outstanding active listings

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
